title: Segment map: starter discovery trace demo
market_scope_summary: Product workspaces for teams that need to connect discovery, delivery, and launch context instead of keeping each stage in separate disconnected artifacts.
segmentation_logic: Segments are separated by how much process discipline, governance need, and product ops support change the value of a traceable operating model.

segments.name: Early product teams
segments.definition: Small product teams adopting more structured discovery and delivery habits.
segments.core_job: Create enough structure that product work can be reviewed and repeated without adding heavy process too early.
struggle_moments: The team lacks a shared story of what happened during discovery
Artifacts are lightweight and easy to lose
primary_value_drivers: A visible structure for repeated product work
segments.buying_context: Usually adopted by a PM or founder trying to introduce structure without enterprise overhead.
adoption_constraints: Too much ceremony will cause rejection
The starter must feel concrete rather than abstract

posture_fit.rationale: The segment values clarity but may not yet feel enough pain to demand strong traceability.

segments.name: Scaling product organizations
segments.definition: Teams with growing coordination needs and rising handoff overhead.
segments.core_job: Keep product work understandable across contributors, reviews, and downstream delivery without reconstructing the story each time.
struggle_moments: Reviews require manually connecting multiple artifacts
Handoffs between discovery and delivery lose important context
segments.buying_context: Usually adopted by PM operators or product ops leads who want a more disciplined operating model.
adoption_constraints: Needs enough structure to be useful without feeling like process theater
Starter value must be obvious on day one

posture_fit.rationale: This segment values a deep operating model more than a generic workspace.
A sharper workflow wedge can grow here before broader platform claims are needed.

segments.name: Ops-enabled product teams
segments.definition: Teams that already care about process standards and want clearer lifecycle auditability.
segments.core_job: Make product operating standards auditable and reusable across recurring work.
struggle_moments: Standards exist but the evidence chain is not easy to inspect
segments.buying_context: Typically influenced by product ops or PM leadership that wants a durable operating baseline.
adoption_constraints: The starter must show strong traceability quickly
The value must exceed a generic docs template

posture_fit.rationale: This segment wants specialist operating discipline and will accept a more opinionated starter.
